Here is a sample code for a plant model that generates a diagram:
```
<?php
// Define the model class
const model = <myclass>
  <% if attr1 == 'color' %>
    // If the attribute is set to `green` instead of `red`, assign the value to the corresponding attribute
    <%= attr2 == 'color' ? 'green' : 'yellow'
      <% end %>
    // Otherwise, assign the value to the corresponding attribute
    <%= attr1 == 'green' ? '' : ''
    // Add a default value to the element if the attribute is `red` instead of `green`.
    <% end %>
  </%>
</myclass>
```

Here's how you could implement this code:
```python
const plant = '<root>';

// Define the model clas class
const model = <myclass>
  <% if attr1 == 'color' ? 'green' : 'yellow' 
    <%= attr2 !== '' && attr2 !== 'red' && attr2 !== ''
    <% end %>'
   </%>
</myclass>
```
The above code defines the model class `plant` that takes in two parameters: a root node (string) and an optional attribute value (`color`) to set if the attribute is set to `green`. It also includes a check for missing @startuml/@enduml tags, which are used to avoid similar errors.

To get the correct code, you can add the following lines:
```python
<% if attr1 == 'color' ? 'green' : 'yellow' 
    <%= attr2 !== '' && attr2 !== ''
    <% end %>'
   </%>
</myclass>
```
